title: Appliances
description: Adds various gadgets, machines, items and gameplay mechanics. Tries to be close to vanilla principles.
body:
![](https://i.imgur.com/ZNmh28B.png)

[![](https://img.shields.io/badge/CurseForge-curseforge?style=flat&logo=curseforge&labelColor=black&color=f16436&link=https%3A%2F%2Fcurseforge.com%2Fminecraft%2Fmc-mods%2Fappliances)](https://curseforge.com/minecraft/mc-mods/appliances) [![](https://img.shields.io/badge/Modrinth-modrinth?style=flat&logo=modrinth&labelColor=16181c&link=https%3A%2F%2Fmodrinth.com%2Fmod%2Fappliances-mod)](https://modrinth.com/mod/appliances-mod) [![](https://img.shields.io/badge/GitHub-github?style=flat&logo=github&labelColor=010409&color=0d1117&link=https%3A%2F%2Fgithub.com%2FMJRamon%2FAppliances)](https://github.com/MJRamon/Appliances)

_"Appliance: a device or piece of equipment designed to perform a specific task."_

Appliances adds various gadgets, machines, items and gameplay mechanics. Tries to be close to vanilla principles.
Ideal for modded hardcore survival.


## Freezer and Cooler

![](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/WWo09vnL/images/1a8b992342cdb560c288725b7668904cdad20b52.png)
![](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/WWo09vnL/images/59d5c931bc39d5febe878dcf73886bbe0bf6f732.png)

Were created with "[Spoiled](https://modrinth.com/mod/spoiled)" mod in mind, as current mod options that include fridges are lacking, too heavy or do not have needed features. Installing "MrCrayfish's Furniture Mod: Refurbished" or "Cooking for Blockheads" just to have a refrigerator? No more!

Though, by default, Freezer only requires energy, and Cooler only requires coolant to operate, you can enable or disable any features you want for each machine.

*   Configurable energy usage (can be completely disabled). Works with pipes.
*   Configurable coolant usage (can be completely disabled). Works with pipes.
*   Start, stop, close, open, processing sounds (can be disabled separately). _Soothing hum included!_
*   Hoppers and item/fluid pipes support.
*   Configurable inventory size.
*   Comparator signal output, just like chests.
*   Adaptive GUI. Automatically adjusts inventory size, hides energy bar and/or collant panel if any of the features are disabled.
*   Whitelist, blacklist support (using item tags).
*   Can accept only edibles or even only meat.


## Water Condenser

![](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/WWo09vnL/images/e2046512ac47fc120b69c0436b63b6a9ebb57765.png)

Time of using sink from "Cooking for Blockheads" as a water source in your low, mid or high tech contraptions has passed. Presenting to you Water Condenser, with configurable tank properties and ability to hydrate farmland blocks in, as always, configurable set area.

*   Configurable tank properties.
*   Can auto output to adjacent blocks (machines, tanks).
*   Farmland hydration: can be completely disabled; area of hydration is configured horizontally and vertically.
*   Can be controlled with a redstone signal.
*   Supports fluid pipes.

## Landmine

![](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/WWo09vnL/images/bed397446051fdfb52aeb7cec73fb53c182c66c1.png)

If you want a landmine that truely suits your needs; if you were installing "Security Craft" only to have mines at your disposal; if you are looking for a solution, — you've came to a right place. As always, highly configurable, landmines have these features:

*   Falls down if there's no block beneath. Can be configured to explode on landing.
*   Flammable - keep away from fire sources!
*   Visual feedback for inactive, active, primed states, with animated blinking state.
*   Chain reaction!
*   Pletora of custom sounds for various events.
*   Can emit light and beeping sound when blinking (configurable).
*   Toggleable block destruction.
*   Toggleable redstone activation.
*   Toggleable fire after explosion. Who said napalm?
*   Configurable grace period, proximity radius, blinking interval, detonation delay.
*   Configurable explosion radius (in Minecraft terms almost the same as amount of damage).


## Tools

![](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/WWo09vnL/images/9ae587f34ef0588f45c1b6e498ee5071e389c077.png)

Have you ever started a new game or went on an adventure and thought: "Man, I need shears. Time to mine for iron"? Or all you want is to light a candle or an extinguished campfire, but now you need to look not only for iron, but flint as well? You don't have to! "Bow Drill" is a cheap alternative to Flint and Steel, yet it has less durability, takes some time and not very reliable. But it surely works when in need. And Wooden and Flint Shears are balanced low and mid tier alternatives to vanilla Shears, having less durability.

As always, everything can be configured!


# Configs

Install "[Configured](https://www.curseforge.com/minecraft/mc-mods/configured)" mod to be able to change configs in-game. World or game restart are required after changes being applied.

# Plans, Help and Suggestions

Have an idea? Suggest in the comments or at [GitHub](https://github.com/MJRamon/Appliances/issues). Currently there are no plans to port to Fabric, NeoForge or support other Minecraft versions, but you can [commit a pull request](https://github.com/MJRamon/Appliances/pulls).

More features are coming.

# Legal

Yes, you can include this mod in a modpack.
